entities.name: MICROPROPAGATION
entities.description: A tissue culture technique used for rapid asexual in vitro propagation of plants

entities.name: TISSUE CULTURE TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Laboratory methods used for the growth of plant cells, tissues, or organs on synthetic culture media

entities.name: ASEXUAL IN VITRO PROPAGATION
entities.description: The process of producing new plants from vegetative parts in a sterile, controlled environment

entities.name: GRAIN LEGUME CROPS
entities.description: A group of crops that produce seeds in pods, including chickpeas

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum L., a major food legume and important pulse crop globally

entities.name: WILD CICER SPECIES
entities.description: Wild relatives of the cultivated chickpea, including C. bijugum, C. cuneatum, C. judaicum, and C. pinnatifidum

entities.name: MULTIPLE SHOOTS
entities.description: The induction and growth of several shoots from a single plant explant

entities.name: SHOOT-TIP EXPLANTS
entities.description: Small segments of shoot tips (2-8 cm long) used as starting material for tissue culture

entities.name: LIGHT REGIMES
entities.description: Specific lighting conditions applied to the cultured explants

entities.name: CHICKPEA GENOTYPES
entities.description: Specific cultivated varieties of chickpea, including L 550, K 850, ICCC 32 (ICCV 6), ICC 12237, ICCC 42, and C 235
